notes:
Runs as native. Only minor problem is that the game freezes on exit.
Unofficial patches work with the steam version under proton.
title: Vampire: The Masquerade - Bloodlines

notes: Latest version of Proton, (4.2-3) borked this game for me, couldn't get to the main menu even. Switching back to 3.16-9 fixed it though. Try 3.16 if you have issues with 4.2.
title: Vampire: The Masquerade - Bloodlines

notes: As previous person stated it does not work with the latest Proton 4.2.3 - The menu ends up garbled after the intro videos. Works perfectly with 3.16.4 however I have to manually kill Vampire.exe after quitting each time.
title: Vampire: The Masquerade - Bloodlines

notes: Works perfectly after applying the unofficial patch. The process has to be killed manually after exiting the game.
title: Vampire: The Masquerade - Bloodlines

notes: Like the former two comments stated, if you launch the game after a fresh install with proton 4.2, the main menu will not be correctly rendered, switching to any version of proton 3 will allow it to load properly. That said, if you install the unofficial patch and set the resolution of the game properly to your screens native resolution, then switch back to proton 4.2, it will run perfectly fine, and this is how I have bee nrunning it for a lot of time now without issue. More info on the github issue page for this game.
title: Vampire: The Masquerade - Bloodlines

notes: Still doesn't work with 4.2 proton, has to be launched with Proton 3.x-x. I still consider it to be "out of the box". Unofficial patch works when using the provided loader-linux.sh.
title: Vampire: The Masquerade - Bloodlines

notes:
As previously stated, i used this guide to install the unofficial patch: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/1804
After installation i had to type "-game Unofficial_Patch" in the SET LAUNCH OPTIONS of the game, and it works great.
Won't shut down properly, have to kill app every time.
Also, it has happened 2 times to me now, the game frozen and couldn't reload, had to fresh re-install. However if you back-up saves from the local files "unofficial patch" folder, you do not need to worry about losing progress in game. This bug has happened both times due to external reasons (system pop-ups, etc).
title: Vampire: The Masquerade - Bloodlines

notes: Still pretty much broken, after the intro movies there is just black screen with menu music for a few seconds. Still leaves processes that need to be killed manually. Tested with unofficial patch and PROTON_USE_D9VK=1
title: Vampire: The Masquerade - Bloodlines
